Frostbringer  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 22
DMG: 12 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 7
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Frostbite (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 40
WT: 2.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

tho its dps may stink compared to weapons nowadays, it is vastly underlooked as an agro weapon. the -15 ac for minute + creats alot of agro ( well im assuming this ) for ive used obsidian scimitar of war alot, and it has a 30dd with less -ac debuff than this, and it holds agro incredibly well. now i have anger 3 in it and my other weapons, but i would say it is on par with an anger 1-2 aug atleast. so this might just hold agro as well as anger 2 maby 3 augs.

▲ Page top
frosty debate
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2003 at 9:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759972317
6 posts
My lvl 53 rng uses a frosty primary and a defender's secondary. This works very well in grps. When going solo I can switch them or leave them, the dmg averages out either way. The frosty will proc more as the primary though.

The combo is one of the better ones. Ideally, you want something fast in primary and something punishing (defender's works well here, so does centi LS) in the offhand.

Lastly, if you have a ranger, have the spell jolt and STILL have problems w/ agro, you need to find a new class.
▲ Page top

Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Jan 14 2003 at 1:56 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591774776190
7 posts
Ima ranger currently using a defiance in offhand and a centi longsword in main.i never have a problem with agro.Was wondering if this would be a good upgrade for my main hand or if it would be to agroey?If not any suggestions and a new main hand weapon?
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Apr 01 2003 at 11:56 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts


Edited, Tue Apr 1 11:20:55 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Mar 03 2003 at 12:34 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591776300308
4 posts
Frosty is an excellent weapon but when used with any set of weapons you must find a balance.

Suggestions:

Primary: Frosty (4-6k)
Secondary: Black Acrylia Long Sword (5-6k) (stuns and heals you by tapping mob hp)

If you notice you require heals switch Black Acryl LS to primary for more processing.

Primary: Frosty
Secondary: Crescent Blades of Luclin (6-8k) (Shadow Step +19AC)

Primary: Frosty
Secondary: Centi LS (2k), Defiance (1.5-2k), etc.

The challenge to dealing damage is to hold agro as MT and not agro but damage as much as possible when not the MT. Sounds simple in theory but will vary depending on group, capabilities of MT and player style both yours and MT's.

The idea is to find the balance -- ride the edge.

As the non MT this is your reason for being in the group. Your job is to whittle down the mobs fast and very infrequently or never require a heal, thus not use mana enabling the group to move to next mob.

Groups with a good damage dealers will appreciate your work and notice no heals for you and that mobs get whittled -- fast.

Finding the right weapon combo in the group your in at the time keeps up the challenge and fun. Even if you only have 3 weapons to pick from switch them between primary and secondary and assess how well MT is holding agro.

Btw, you want processes pump up the Dex. Look at Bracelet of Quickness (+15Dex 8-10k), Bracer of Agitation (+9Dex 700-800pp), Mushroom Veil +12Dex 600-1k), Valorium Ring of Galant (+8 Dex 6-8k), Vah Shir Claw Earring (+6Dex 1.5-2.5k) and armor items with Dex.

Other weapons to consider with or instead of Frostbringer:

Tae Ew Long Sword 12/20 (8-10k)
Snow Chipper 10/20 (8-12k) (next tier up with great stats and +3%Parry mod)

As you increase your damage output you can group with higher and meaner MTs and literally be the killer asset to the group.

Happy Hunting !
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply

# Mar 11 2003 at 11:15 PM Rating: Decent

Very well said :) good thing you gave much closer pricing to those items then this sites pricing guide suggests, the items you listed, for the most part, were right on the money for my servers PC atm.

Anyways I just made a ranger and have yet to group with anyone, but I love your suggestions and how you provided good examples of each combo (leeching w/ black acrylia, shadow step proc, etc..). Thanks for that post :)
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2003 at 7:10 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Depends on the group you're with. You'll do a lot more damage with Defiance in your primary (even with the Centi LS), and Frostbringer in your off-hand but you'll also generate a lot more agro. Just keep Jolt handy. If you're grouped with competent tanks, aggro won't be an issue.
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Mar 17 2003 at 9:47 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761297211
119 posts
"If you're grouped with competent tanks, aggro won't be an issue."

A tank has basically 2 things to control aggro...distance from mob and taunt...after that the competancy falls to the dmg dealers and not the tank. Its their job to not take aggro not his to ensure that they dont. Any class in game can take aggro faster/better than a warrior, the real trick is to not take it!

Gammal
55 warrrior
▲ Page top
RE: Glad to know some know duel weilding rules
QuoteReply
# Mar 23 2003 at 12:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760083053
78 posts
well said. to add fuel to your fire its the reason behind skills like feign death and evade : ).

QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2002 at 7:18 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570776553
40 posts
why can't ppl read than post message?
i see like 10 messages alone for the proc lvl P""!
anyway back to topic
this is a great weapon for a warr and not a ranger since it creates a lot of agro from the armour debuff
if you have the money for frosty as a ranger...i would recommand you to buy a snowchipper instead(that is if you are 51+) OR a steel hilted flint dagger...it has a 625proc and with 150+ base dex it procs massively(HoS + chanter haste would make you do immense dmg)in KC a few nights back our monk pulled 3 mobs. i alone did 5k+ dmg with the dagger and woulda wiped out if i didn't have that dagger(cleric lom + monk dead)

# Mar 29 2003 at 3:29 PM Rating: Default

▲ Page top
re: not to warriors
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2002 at 12:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248401364
10 posts
The warrior was 4 levels lower than the ranger and 2 levels lower than monk...warrior will almost never outtaunt them in that situation. Especially if monk is epic. Monks and rangers usually outdamage warriors. Verant designed the endgame this way. For informative discussion of this see the SteelWarrior board. Monk does 2x damage of warrior.
"Aggro management" is the best verant could do to make mob encounters challenging. If the warrior had better taunt or damage, they reasoned, all encounters would be unchallenging b/c if it was easy to hold aggro, everyone would just slaughter the mob without thinking.
▲ Page top
Not to Warriors wanting this for the Taunt
QuoteReply
# Sep 16 2002 at 9:00 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591774066779
Scholar
103 posts
Just a word of advice to the lower(ish) warriors that think getting this will automaticly shoot your agro through the roof. A Frosty CAN be a really good source of agro, IF you have good dex (like 150+).
Example from KC about a week ago: Group of Warrior/Ranger(me)/Monk/Shammy/Chanter/Cleric. I don't remember casters levels but i remember I was 55, Warrior was 51, monk was 53, those are the important ones. Warrior had Guard Captains Mallet (11/20) + Frosty (12/22), I had Lupine Dagger (9/18) + JBoM (13/25), Monk had a T-Staff (29/30). After HoS my dex was 215, warriors dex was 150ish (153 i think), monk was 180ish. Call of Fire was going off rather often (65DD +stun) ranger 55 proc buff, while the warriors Stun hammer and frosty were barely firing at all, and the monks Tstaff was going off a good bit as well. Needless to say, I was laying on the Jolt hotkey, while the monk was FDing left and right. Warrior was having a rough time holding agro. When the Frosty DID go off, she held the agro fine though.
Moral of this story, If you're gonna invest in a Frosty, make sure you invest in some good dex gear 1st, it'll save your friendly neighborhood rangers, rogues, and monks a LOT of agro negation. ;)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 25 2003 at 4:58 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) "while the warriors Stun hammer and frosty were barely firing at all"
RE: Not to Warriors wanting this for the Taunt
QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2002 at 7:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570776553
40 posts
just one thing
call of sky is only 35DD + 0 second stun
not 65....also the stun is only for interrupting spell, not much taunt
just wanted to point that out...prolly just typo(i hope so..he is lvl 55!) but 30 more dmg is much more much more taunt
▲ Page top
RE: Not to Warriors wanting this for the Taunt
QuoteReply
# Sep 18 2002 at 11:58 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592252462989
View User Forum
Sage
211 posts
Eeeeks. Sounds to me like the warrior really needs to invest in some dex gear hon. At level 55 a warrior really needs to think through how he is going to maintain aggro for the group -- looking for tht equipment that will not only add to AC and HP, but critical stats as well.

This post is more of a huggles post to her long term friend Wolv :-)

A level 55 war that has HoS and only has a dex of 150? Not bloody likely unless she just did a server transfer. At 55 my base dex, and not that well equipped was 135 so with HoS 185. Now running 185 base... much better equipped.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Aug 22 2002 at 2:24 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591774595691
View User Forum
114 posts
Got one for my 45 ranger main for 5k! sum1 in bazaar had 1 for 8k and 5500. I sent the guy for 5500pp a tell asking if he accepted trades and 5k and he said i'll just take 5k. i use this and defiance and i hit fast and with str self buffed at 182 its decent damage also i haev SCHW and so get the good haste. I find soloing mobs is easier hehe. altho cos i mostly have str items i have low sta and therefore low hp. Altho i have AC 872 self buffed i still have trouble any non animal mobs can get tough and can see me rooting them and steppiong back to heal a little. But in a group where i dont tank i tend to out damage a war ) altho saving up for a LSoEE now so i can put FB into primary and LSoEE into off hand. After my monk gets more twinked hehe`

Packs a lot of taunt, saving up some money to buy mine. Used a guildy's earlier today in King group in Sebilis, didn't get any ping pong agro at all like I normally do when using my Crafted Velium Warsword

#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 10 2002 at 1:49 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I FIGHT WITH MY DAUGHTER AND SHAMAN PARTNER FOR 56 LVLS. NO GUILDES. THESE GUYS BOUGHT A FROSTY FOR ME FOR BIRTHDAY. SINCE NO GUILDE LOL THATS A LOTTA IGS LOL. THIS WEAPON IN MY MAIN WITH A BLOODFIRE 11/24 650 DOT SECONDARY AND I REALLY DO THINK ONLY EPIC WILL BE CLOSE. FOR KILLER GRAPHIC LOOKS I PUT IN GREEN JADED BROAD AXE WITH FROSTY. WITH SHAMANS CELERITY MY DAUGHTERS LAMMYS USED TO PULL TAUNT AWAY. NOW WITH FROSTY MOBS STAY PUT, REALLY, AN AWESOME WEAPON. GET ONE AND KEEP TILL EQ ENDS YOU HAVE A CLASSIC). DAININ AND LILFOX THANK YAS SO MUCH. TO ALL PEACE AND SEE YA ON LANYS. GLAIN 56TH WARRIOR.
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 03 2002 at 3:17 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) HOLY COW IS YOUR CAPS BROKE OR WHAT?
Excellent 1hs
QuoteReply
# Jul 07 2002 at 5:30 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759517522
21 posts
So far, I've thoroughly enjoyed this weapon. For starters, 12 22 is a great damage ratio, better than even lamentation and other competitors. I currently use this in the main paired up with velium swiftblade in my offhand. I would consider this a wise choice at 40, because that is when you're frostbringer will start to proc and when your VSB will reach its maximum haste. At around 100 dex I have gotten this to proc up to 4 times against a green mob (barely green). I highly recommend this to all dual wielding classes, due to its extreme damage ratio and occasional proc that can really turn the outcome of a battle.
▲ Page top
RE: Excellent 1hs
QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2003 at 11:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778448322
Scholar
48 posts
Haste is NOT based on level. That misconception is made because clicking on the "Haste" link on allakhazam brings you to the Spell: Haste, which is based on level.
